title: podocarpus matudae subsp. macrocarpus
intro: Podocarpus matudae subsp. macrocarpus, also known as the Mexican yew or Mexican podocarpus, is a coniferous plant belonging to the Podocarpaceae family. This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Podocarpus matudae subsp. macrocarpus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of infections and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
